_meta.description: Per-field documentation for POST /models/load `options` object. Sourced from sd.cpp (include/stable-diffusion.h + .cpp consumers); keep these in sync with the C++ ModelLoadParams struct.

n_threads.label: CPU Threads
n_threads.description: Number of CPU threads for model operations (loading, mmap fault-in, conditioner forward passes when keep_clip_on_cpu is set). Set to -1 for automatic detection (uses std::thread::hardware_concurrency()).
n_threads.recommended: Leave at -1 (auto). Lower it only if you're sharing the box with other CPU-heavy workloads.

keep_clip_on_cpu.label: Keep CLIP on CPU
keep_clip_on_cpu.description: Keep the CLIP / T5 / LLM text encoder on CPU instead of GPU. Saves substantial VRAM (T5-XXL alone is ~9 GiB fp16) at the cost of slightly slower text-encoding (one pass per generation, runs once before the diffusion loop).
keep_clip_on_cpu.recommended: Enable for SD3 / Flux / Z-Image / Qwen-Image on GPUs with less than 16 GiB VRAM.

keep_vae_on_cpu.label: Keep VAE on CPU
keep_vae_on_cpu.description: Keep the VAE on CPU. The VAE is idle during the entire sampler loop and only needed at the very end (decode), so the impact is one slow decode step per image.
keep_vae_on_cpu.recommended: Enable only if you're VRAM-bound during VAE decode. With layer_streaming + max_vram, sd.cpp parks the VAE on CPU automatically.

keep_controlnet_on_cpu.label: Keep ControlNet on CPU
keep_controlnet_on_cpu.description: Keep ControlNet weights on CPU. ControlNet runs every diffusion step, so this slows generation roughly proportional to ControlNet's size — use only if ControlNet is what tips you over the VRAM limit.
keep_controlnet_on_cpu.recommended: Enable when running ControlNet alongside large diffusion models on smaller GPUs.

flash_attn.label: Flash Attention (CLIP / T5)
flash_attn.description: Enable Flash Attention for the conditioner (CLIP / T5 / LLM). Faster and lower-VRAM attention. CUDA-only.
flash_attn.recommended: Keep on. Disable only if you suspect numerical issues in the conditioner.

diffusion_flash_attn.label: Flash Attention (Diffusion)
diffusion_flash_attn.description: Enable Flash Attention specifically for the diffusion model (UNet / DiT / Flux / MMDiT). sd.cpp keeps this independent from `flash_attn` because CUDA support and numerical stability used to differ between the two.
diffusion_flash_attn.recommended: Try on for SDXL / Flux / SD3 / Z-Image. Bench against off — usually faster, occasionally produces different outputs at the per-pixel level.

offload_to_cpu.label: Offload Params to CPU
offload_to_cpu.description: Keep model parameters in CPU RAM and stream them to GPU on demand (sd.cpp's static `offload_params_to_cpu`). Different from the dynamic `offload_mode` below — this is set once at model-load and stays for the model's lifetime.
offload_to_cpu.recommended: Enable when the model file is larger than your VRAM but fits in RAM. Significantly slower than VRAM-resident inference.

enable_mmap.label: Memory-mapped Loading
enable_mmap.description: mmap() the model file instead of read()-ing it into anonymous memory. Much faster initial load (no full file read) and the kernel can evict pages under memory pressure.
enable_mmap.recommended: Keep on. Disable only on filesystems that don't handle large mmaps well (some network mounts).

vae_decode_only.label: VAE Decode Only
vae_decode_only.description: Skip loading the VAE encoder weights — only the decoder is loaded. Saves ~50% of VAE memory but img2img and inpainting break (no encode path). Image-edit via `ref_images` still works because that doesn't go through the VAE encoder.
vae_decode_only.recommended: Keep on for txt2img-only workflows. Disable for img2img / inpainting.

vae_tiling.label: VAE Tiling
vae_tiling.description: Process the VAE in spatial tiles instead of as one large tensor. Reduces peak VAE-decode VRAM dramatically (the encoder/decoder are the most memory-hungry parts). Mild quality cost from tile boundaries.
vae_tiling.recommended: Enable for high-resolution generation (≥2048 px) or whenever VAE decode OOMs.

vae_tile_size_x.label: VAE Tile Width
vae_tile_size_x.description: Width of each VAE tile in pixels. 0 = sd.cpp picks a default based on architecture (typically 512–768).
vae_tile_size_x.recommended: Leave at 0 (auto). Tune only if you see visible tile seams — try 768 or 1024.

vae_tile_size_y.label: VAE Tile Height
vae_tile_size_y.description: Height of each VAE tile in pixels. 0 = auto.

vae_tile_overlap.label: VAE Tile Overlap
vae_tile_overlap.description: Fraction of overlap between adjacent VAE tiles, used to blend seams (0.0–1.0). Higher = fewer seam artifacts, more compute.
vae_tile_overlap.recommended: 0.5 is a safe default. Drop to 0.25 for speed if seams aren't visible; raise to 0.75 if they are.

vae_conv_direct.label: VAE Direct Convolution
vae_conv_direct.description: Use ggml_conv2d_direct (im2col + matmul) in the VAE instead of the FFT-based path. Faster on some GPUs (notably consumer-grade RTX), slower on others.
vae_conv_direct.recommended: Z-Image: enable. Other architectures: bench it; effect varies by GPU and image size.

diffusion_conv_direct.label: Diffusion Direct Convolution
diffusion_conv_direct.description: Same as `vae_conv_direct` but for the diffusion model. Affects every sampler step.
diffusion_conv_direct.recommended: Bench against off on your hardware. Effect is small but consistent — pick the faster one and stick with it.

tae_preview_only.label: TAESD Preview Only
tae_preview_only.description: Use TAESD (Tiny AutoEncoder) only for live preview frames during sampling — the final image still goes through the full VAE. TAESD weights are ~10 MiB so the cost is trivial.
tae_preview_only.recommended: Enable when you're loading a TAESD model just for previews and want full-quality finals.

free_params_immediately.label: Free Params Immediately
free_params_immediately.description: Free model weights from GPU memory immediately after generation completes. Useful for one-shot CLIs but cripples a server (every generation pays the full reload cost).
free_params_immediately.recommended: Keep off for server use. Was the right default for sd.cpp's CLI; it's wrong for restapi.

weight_type.label: Weight Type
weight_type.description: Override the precision used for model weights at runtime. Lower precision uses less VRAM but may affect quality. Empty string = use the model's native precision (read from the safetensors / GGUF header).

values.bf16: Brain float 16 — same memory as f16 with wider exponent range
values.q4_k: 4-bit K-quants — smaller than q4_0 with better quality

weight_type.recommended: Leave on Auto. Override only when the source file is f32 / f16 and you want to force a smaller runtime size.

tensor_type_rules.label: Per-tensor Weight Rules
tensor_type_rules.description: sd.cpp regex rules for per-tensor precision overrides, e.g. `^vae\.=f16` to force the VAE to fp16 even when the rest of the model is q4. Multiple rules are comma-separated.
tensor_type_rules.recommended: Empty unless you know the tensor naming convention. See sd.cpp's `--tensor-type-rules` documentation.

rng_type.label: RNG Type
rng_type.description: Random number generator backend used to draw initial noise. `cuda` produces output that matches comfyui / diffusers on NVIDIA. `cpu` is deterministic across hardware but slower at start-of-generation. `std_default` is C++ std::mt19937.

values.cuda: CUDA RNG — matches comfyui / diffusers on NVIDIA
values.cpu: CPU RNG — deterministic across hardware, slightly slower

rng_type.recommended: Keep `cuda` to match ecosystem outputs. Use `cpu` if you need reproducibility across different GPUs.

sampler_rng_type.label: Sampler RNG Type
sampler_rng_type.description: RNG used by stochastic samplers (DPM++ ancestral, etc.). Empty = same as `rng_type`. Separating the two lets you have CUDA-RNG initial noise but CPU-RNG sampler steps for partial reproducibility.

prediction.label: Prediction Type
prediction.description: Override sd.cpp's auto-detection of the model's prediction objective. Empty = auto-detect from the safetensors header / model class.
prediction.recommended: Leave on Auto. Override only when sd.cpp misdetects (rare; happens with custom-trained models that lack the usual metadata).

lora_apply_mode.label: LoRA Apply Mode
lora_apply_mode.description: When LoRAs are merged into the base model. `auto` lets sd.cpp decide based on the model architecture. `immediately` merges at load time (faster generation, slower load, can't change LoRAs without reload). `at_runtime` keeps LoRAs separate and applies them per generation (faster load, slightly slower generation, can swap LoRAs without reloading the model).

values.immediately: Immediately — merge into weights at load (fast generation, immutable)
values.at_runtime: At runtime — apply per-generation (mutable, ~5% slower per step)

lora_apply_mode.recommended: `at_runtime` for server use — lets generation requests change LoRAs without reloading the model.

force_sdxl_vae_conv_scale.label: Force SDXL VAE Conv Scale
force_sdxl_vae_conv_scale.description: Apply SDXL-specific VAE convolution scaling unconditionally (sd.cpp normally autodetects). Internal compatibility flag for SDXL VAEs that lack the scale metadata.
force_sdxl_vae_conv_scale.recommended: Off. Enable only if you have a stripped SDXL VAE that's producing washed-out outputs.

max_vram.label: Max VRAM (GiB)
max_vram.description: GiB budget for graph-cut segmented param offload (PR #1476). When set, sd.cpp partitions the diffusion graph so peak weight residency stays under this budget. Lives on `sd_ctx_params_t` and is independent of `offload_mode` — they can be combined. 0 = disabled.
max_vram.recommended: 0 (disabled) by default. Set to slightly less than your total VRAM (e.g. 10 on a 12 GiB card) when the model still OOMs at the smallest sample step. Combines well with `offload_mode = layer_streaming`.

offload_mode.label: Dynamic VRAM Offloading
offload_mode.description: Cross-stage offload strategy. Different from the static `offload_to_cpu` — these dynamically move components between CPU and GPU during a generation.

values.none: Disabled — keep all components on GPU (fastest, needs most VRAM)
values.cond_only: After Conditioning — offload text encoders after the prompt pass (good balance for small VRAM gains)
values.cond_diffusion: After Cond + Diffusion — also offload diffusion after sampling, keeps VAE on GPU
values.aggressive: Aggressive — offload each component when not in use (saves most VRAM, slowest)
values.layer_streaming: Layer Streaming — stream UNet/DiT layers one-by-one (enables models larger than VRAM)

offload_mode.recommended: `cond_only` for 12 GiB GPUs running Z-Image / Flux. `layer_streaming` when the model file alone is bigger than your VRAM.

vram_estimation.label: VRAM Estimation Method
vram_estimation.description: How sd.cpp predicts the peak VRAM each component needs. `dryrun` builds a throwaway compute graph for an exact answer. `formula` uses a closed-form heuristic — faster, less precise.

values.formula: Formula — closed-form heuristic (faster, may over- or under-estimate)

vram_estimation.recommended: `dryrun` for accuracy. Switch to `formula` only if you find dryrun's overhead noticeable on small generations.

offload_cond_stage.label: Offload LLM/CLIP After Cond
offload_cond_stage.description: Move the text encoder (LLM for Z-Image, CLIP/T5 for SD3/Flux) to CPU after the prompt is encoded. Makes the text encoder available for the next generation but lets the diffusion model reclaim the freed VRAM.
offload_cond_stage.recommended: On when using any `offload_mode` other than `none`.

offload_diffusion.label: Offload Diffusion After Sampling
offload_diffusion.description: Move the diffusion model (UNet/DiT) to CPU after the sampler loop finishes, before VAE decode runs. Lets VAE decode use the full GPU.
offload_diffusion.recommended: Enable in `cond_diffusion` or `aggressive` modes when VAE decode is what's hitting OOM.

reload_cond_stage.label: Reload LLM/CLIP After Generation
reload_cond_stage.description: After generation, copy the text encoder back to GPU so the next generation starts faster. Holds VRAM between generations — disable if you want max VRAM available for non-generation work between requests.
reload_cond_stage.recommended: On for server use (multiple generations). Off for one-shot CLIs.

reload_diffusion.label: Reload Diffusion After Generation
reload_diffusion.description: Same logic as `reload_cond_stage` but for the diffusion model.

log_offload_events.label: Log Offload Events
log_offload_events.description: sd.cpp logs each offload / reload event to stdout. Useful for diagnosing slow generations (see how often things are moving) but spammy in production.
log_offload_events.recommended: On while tuning. Off in steady-state production once you've calibrated.

min_offload_size_mb.label: Min Offload Size (MB)
min_offload_size_mb.description: Skip offloading any component smaller than this many megabytes. Prevents wasting PCIe bandwidth moving tiny tensors that wouldn't actually free meaningful VRAM. 0 = no minimum.
min_offload_size_mb.recommended: 0 to start. Raise to 256 if you see lots of noise in the offload log for tiny components (TAESD, conditioner sub-modules).

target_free_vram_mb.label: Target Free VRAM (MB)
target_free_vram_mb.description: VRAM headroom sd.cpp tries to keep free before VAE decode. 0 = always offload (when mode is set). Higher = trigger offloading sooner.
target_free_vram_mb.recommended: 0 unless you're seeing decode OOM despite aggressive offloading — then bump to 1024 or 2048.

streaming_prefetch_layers.label: Layer Streaming: Prefetch Ahead
streaming_prefetch_layers.description: When `offload_mode = layer_streaming`, how many layers ahead to start copying to GPU before they're needed. Higher = more overlap of compute and PCIe transfer (faster) but more VRAM in flight.
streaming_prefetch_layers.recommended: 1 is the sd.cpp default. Try 2 if PCIe bandwidth is your bottleneck and you have headroom.

streaming_keep_layers_behind.label: Layer Streaming: Keep Behind
streaming_keep_layers_behind.description: When `offload_mode = layer_streaming`, how many already-executed layers to keep resident on GPU. Useful for skip connections (UNet U-shape) where the encoder side's outputs feed the decoder later in the same step.
streaming_keep_layers_behind.recommended: 0 for DiT (Flux/SD3/Z-Image — purely linear graph). UNet (SDXL/SD1.x) may benefit from 1–2 to avoid re-streaming for skip connections.

streaming_min_free_vram_mb.label: Layer Streaming: Min Free VRAM (MB)
streaming_min_free_vram_mb.description: Minimum VRAM sd.cpp tries to keep free during layer streaming. Backstop for the prefetch window — if streaming would push residency above (total - this), it stalls until the next layer's done.
streaming_min_free_vram_mb.recommended: 0 (disabled) unless you're co-running other GPU workloads and need predictable headroom.

chroma_use_dit_mask.label: Chroma DiT Mask
chroma_use_dit_mask.description: Enable the DiT-side attention masking that Chroma was trained with. Required for correct Chroma outputs.
chroma_use_dit_mask.recommended: Keep on for Chroma; ignored for other architectures.

chroma_use_t5_mask.label: Chroma T5 Mask
chroma_use_t5_mask.description: Enable T5-side attention masking on Chroma. Reduces hallucinated tokens at the cost of slightly different prompt fidelity.
chroma_use_t5_mask.recommended: Off (matches sd.cpp default). Try on if Chroma is producing artifacts in long prompts.

chroma_t5_mask_pad.label: Chroma T5 Mask Padding
chroma_t5_mask_pad.description: Padding token count appended to T5 prompts when `chroma_use_t5_mask` is on. Compensates for Chroma's training-time padding behavior.
chroma_t5_mask_pad.recommended: Default 1. Bump only if Chroma docs explicitly say so for your fine-tune.

flow_shift.label: Flow Shift (default)
flow_shift.description: Default flow-matching shift parameter for Flux / SD3 / Z-Image models. INFINITY (sent as nothing or omitted) = auto-detect based on model. This is the LOAD-time default; the per-generation request can override.
flow_shift.recommended: Leave unset (auto). Override only when you're tuning for a specific model that benefits from a non-default shift.
